投訴信範本
一人一信去投訴

Complain about the misbehaviour of your staff

To whom it may concern,

I am writing to express my concern about the behaviours of your staff.

Calvin Chen, Louis XIII Brand Ambassador in Hong Kong, posted an inappropriate post in his social media Facebook. He described the Hong Kong protestors as ‘cockroaches’ on 23rd September 2019. Also, he set a logo of well-known insecticide product “Raid” as his profile picture and a picture, which has some disgusting dead cockroaches on the floor, as his cover photo.

Therefore, I believe your staff is supporting violence and dehumanisation.

I am worrying about the safety of protestors and I am not sure what is he going to do. Will he attack the protestors randomly in the street? This may be criminal so I am considering to report this to Hong Kong Police Force.

Supporting dehumanisation is totally unacceptable in 21st century because dehumanisation was used by Hutu before they murdered Tutsi in 1994 Rwandan genocide. Thus, your staff should not support this terrorism.

Finally, I would like to know the opinion of the company. Are you supporting violence and dehumanisation? If not, I think the behaviours of your staff is not matched with the policies of the company. Please consider to terminate the employment with him. I will also send this to United Nations Human Rights Council, United States House of Representatives and United States Senates as a reference or record in Hong Kong Human Rights and Democracy Act.

Best regards,
HongKongers

quote.msg:
投訴信範本
一人一信去投訴

Complain about the misbehaviour of your staff

To whom it may concern,

I am writing to express my concern about the behaviours of your staff.

Calvin Chen, Louis XIII Brand Ambassador in Hong Kong, posted an inappropriate post in his social media Facebook. He described the Hong Kong protestors as ‘cockroaches’ on 23rd September 2019. Also, he set a logo of well-known insecticide product “Raid” as his profile picture and a picture, which has some disgusting dead cockroaches on the floor, as his cover photo.

Therefore, I believe your staff is supporting violence and dehumanisation.

I am worrying about the safety of protestors and I am not sure what is he going to do. Will he attack the protestors randomly in the street? This may be criminal so I am considering to report this to Hong Kong Police Force.

Supporting dehumanisation is totally unacceptable in 21st century because dehumanisation was used by Hutu before they murdered Tutsi in 1994 Rwandan genocide. Thus, your staff should not support this terrorism.

Finally, I would like to know the opinion of the company. Are you supporting violence and dehumanisation? If not, I think the behaviours of your staff is not matched with the policies of the company. Please consider to terminate the employment with him. I will also send this to United Nations Human Rights Council, United States House of Representatives and United States Senates as a reference or record in Hong Kong Human Rights and Democracy Act.

Best regards,
HongKongers
